title: Valley of Peshawar

infobox.value: ([Pashto](/wiki/Pashto_language "Pashto language"): د لوی پېښور وادي; [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): وادئ پشاور) is a broad area situated in the central part of the [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa") province of [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"). The valley is 7,176 km2 (2,771 sq mi) in area, and is traversed by the [Kabul River](/wiki/Kabul_River "Kabul River"). It has a mean elevation of 345 metres (1,132 ft).[[1]](#cite_note-1) The valley takes its name from the city of [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), which is situated at the western part of the valley close to [Warsak Dam](/wiki/Warsak_Dam "Warsak Dam"). To the west of the valley lies the [Khyber Pass](/wiki/Khyber_Pass "Khyber Pass"). The five most populous cities in the valley are Peshawar, [Mardan](/wiki/Mardan "Mardan"), [Swabi](/wiki/Swabi "Swabi"), [Charsadda](/wiki/Charsadda "Charsadda"), and [Nowshera](/wiki/Nowshera,_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Nowshera, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa").

Samad, Rafi U. (2011). [*The Grandeur of Gandhara: The Ancient Buddhist Civilization of the Swat, Peshawar, Kabul and Indus Valleys*](https://books.google.com/books?id=cJtMBAAAQBAJ&dq=ancient+peshawar&pg=PA18). Algora Publishing. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9780875868585](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780875868585 "Special:BookSources/9780875868585"). Retrieved 24 March 2017.

["DISTRICT AND TEHSIL LEVEL POPULATION SUMMARY WITH REGION BREAKUP: KHYBER PAKHTUNKHWA"](https://i3.edu.pl/i/CHARSADA_SUMMARY.pdf) (PDF). [Pakistan Bureau of Statistics](/wiki/Pakistan_Bureau_of_Statistics "Pakistan Bureau of Statistics"). 2018-01-03. Retrieved 2018-04-23.

Beal, Samuel, (ed. & trans.), (1884). [Si-yu-ki: Buddhist Records of the Wester World, Volume 1](https://ia801607.us.archive.org/1/items/siyukibuddhistre01hsuoft/siyukibuddhistre01hsuoft_bw.pdf), Author: Huen Tsang, **p. 112:** "Outside the eastern gate of the town of Po-lu-sha is a sangharama with about fifty priests, who all study the Great Vehicle. Here is a stupa built by Asoka-raja [...] To the north-east of Po-lu-sha city about 20 li or so we come to Mount Dantaloka. Above a ridge of that mountain is a stupa built by Asoka-raja."

Errington, Elizabeth, (1993). ["In search of Pa-lu-sha, a city of the central Gandhara plain"](https://www.academia.edu/1130923/In_search_of_Pa_lu_sha_a_city_of_the_central_Gandhara_plain), in: Bulletin of the Asia Institute, New Series, Volume 7, p. 63: "[R]egarding the Tan-to-lo-ka mountain visited by Xuanzang 'above twenty li' (4 miles/6.4 km) to the northeast of Pa-lu-sha [...], for if the latter is identified as Sahri Bahlol, it seems that the former must be equated with the Takht-i-Bāhi hill."

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Geographical area in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2a/A_look_back_while_climbing_uphill_at_Ranigat_1.jpg/250px-A_look_back_while_climbing_uphill_at_Ranigat_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:A_look_back_while_climbing_uphill_at_Ranigat_1.jpg)

Peshawar valley seen from [Ranigat](/wiki/Ranigat "Ranigat").

The **Valley of Peshawar** ([Pashto](/wiki/Pashto_language "Pashto language"): د لوی پېښور وادي; [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): وادئ پشاور) is a broad area situated in the central part of the [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa") province of [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"). The valley is 7,176 km2 (2,771 sq mi) in area, and is traversed by the [Kabul River](/wiki/Kabul_River "Kabul River"). It has a mean elevation of 345 metres (1,132 ft).[[1]](#cite_note-1) The valley takes its name from the city of [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), which is situated at the western part of the valley close to [Warsak Dam](/wiki/Warsak_Dam "Warsak Dam"). To the west of the valley lies the [Khyber Pass](/wiki/Khyber_Pass "Khyber Pass"). The five most populous cities in the valley are Peshawar, [Mardan](/wiki/Mardan "Mardan"), [Swabi](/wiki/Swabi "Swabi"), [Charsadda](/wiki/Charsadda "Charsadda"), and [Nowshera](/wiki/Nowshera,_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Nowshera, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa").

## Districts located in the valley

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Valley_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Districts located in the valley")]

These [districts of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Districts_of_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Districts of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa") are completely located in the Valley of Peshawar:

* [Charsadda District](/wiki/Charsadda_District,_Pakistan "Charsadda District, Pakistan") (population: 1,616,198)[[2]](#cite_note-census2017-2)
* [Mardan District](/wiki/Mardan_District "Mardan District") (population: 2,373,061)
* [Peshawar District](/wiki/Peshawar_District "Peshawar District") (population: 4,269,079)

In addition, most of [Nowshera District](/wiki/Nowshera_District "Nowshera District"), most of [Swabi District](/wiki/Swabi_District "Swabi District"), and smaller portions of [Khyber](/wiki/Khyber_District "Khyber District") (including [Jamrud](/wiki/Jamrud "Jamrud")), [Mohmand](/wiki/Mohmand_District "Mohmand District"), [Malakand](/wiki/Malakand_District "Malakand District"), and [Frontier Region Peshawar](/wiki/Frontier_Region_Peshawar "Frontier Region Peshawar") are also located in the Valley of Peshawar.

## Historic references

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Valley_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Historic references")]

In 630 CE, [Xuanzang](/wiki/Xuanzang "Xuanzang") visited the Valley of Peshawar, and mentioned two stupas built by [Ashoka](/wiki/Ashoka "Ashoka"), one of them around (20 li) 6.4 km northeast of (Pa-lu-sha) [Sahri Bahlol](/wiki/Seri_Bahlol "Seri Bahlol"), which he said was located on "Mount Tan-to-lo-ka," [Takht-i-Bāhi](/wiki/Takht-i-Bahi "Takht-i-Bahi") hill.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
